content.content:
diff --git a/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile b/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
index f197870d1..53466af89 100644
--- a/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
+++ b/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
@@ -24,7 +24,7 @@ QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
 SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
 NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
 
-# Assembly sources
+# Assembly sources — single-precision
 ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
            sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
            sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
@@ -32,6 +32,15 @@ ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
            sf_fpclassify.s
 ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
 
+# Assembly sources — double-precision
+S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
+             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
+             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
+             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
+             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
+             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
+S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
+
 # Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
 # standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
 
@@ -40,15 +49,17 @@ FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
                sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
                modff fpclassifyf
 
-# Double-precision functions (must have test_<name>.c; links against toolchain libm)
-FUNCS_DOUBLE = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh \
-               log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
-               acosh atanh expm1 log1p erf erfc lgamma \
-               j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder remquo rint round lrint \
-               lround trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter
+# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
+FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
+                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
+                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
+
+# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
+FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
+                     hypot remainder lrint lround
 
 # All functions to test
-FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)
+FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
 
 # Test output directory
 TESTDIR = test
@@ -86,8 +97,8 @@ $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
 	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
 
 test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt

toolUseResult.stdout:
diff --git a/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile b/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
index f197870d1..53466af89 100644
--- a/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
+++ b/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile
@@ -24,7 +24,7 @@ QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
 SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
 NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
 
-# Assembly sources
+# Assembly sources — single-precision
 ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
            sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
            sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
@@ -32,6 +32,15 @@ ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
            sf_fpclassify.s
 ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
 
+# Assembly sources — double-precision
+S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
+             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
+             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
+             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
+             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
+             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
+S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
+
 # Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
 # standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
 
@@ -40,15 +49,17 @@ FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
                sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
                modff fpclassifyf
 
-# Double-precision functions (must have test_<name>.c; links against toolchain libm)
-FUNCS_DOUBLE = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh \
-               log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
-               acosh atanh expm1 log1p erf erfc lgamma \
-               j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder remquo rint round lrint \
-               lround trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter
+# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
+FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
+                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
+                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
+
+# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
+FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
+                     hypot remainder lrint lround
 
 # All functions to test
-FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)
+FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
 
 # Test output directory
 TESTDIR = test
@@ -86,8 +97,8 @@ $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
 	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
 
 test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt

input.command: git diff test/test_sinf.c 2>/dev/null || git diff test/test_cosf.c | head -80
input.description: Show a test file diff sample

content.content:
printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
 	else \
@@ -96,22 +107,47 @@ test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
 	fi
 endef
 
-# For double-precision functions: build ref ELF only (no assembly optimization)
+# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
+define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
+$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
+	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
+
+$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
+	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
+
+test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
+	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
+	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
+	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
+		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
+	else \
+		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
+			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
+	fi
+endef
+
+# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
 define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
 $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
 	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
 
 test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
-	@printf "  %-14s RUN\n" $(1)
+	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
+	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
+		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
+	else \
+		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
+	fi
 endef
 
 $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
-$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
+$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
+$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
 
 # --- Top-level targets ---
 
-OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
+OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
+           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
 REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
 
 all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
@@ -119,20 +155,20 @@ all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
 test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
 	@echo "Done."
 
-size: $(ASM_OBJS)
+size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
 	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
-	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
+	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
 		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
 			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
 	@echo "  --------------------"
 	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
-		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
+		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
 
 list:
 	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
 	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
 
 clean:
-	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
+	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
 
 .PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \

@@ -96,22 +107,47 @@ test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf

+		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
+	else \

+			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
+	fi
+endef

+# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)

 	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

-	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
-	@printf "  %-14s RUN\n" $(1)
+	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
+	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
+		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
+	else \
+		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
+	fi
 endef
 
 $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
-$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
+$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
+$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
 
 # --- Top-level targets ---
 
-OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
+OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
+           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
 REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
 
 all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
@@ -119,20 +155,20 @@ all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
 test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
 	@echo "Done."
 
-size: $(ASM_OBJS)
+size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
 	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
-	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
+	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
 		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
 			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
 	@echo "  --------------------"
 	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
-		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
+		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
 
 list:
 	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
 	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
 
 clean:
-	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
+	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
 
 .PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text:
The changes are:
1. **Makefile** - added double-precision assembly (s_*.s) support with opt vs ref comparison
2. **test/test_*f.c** - expanded single-precision tests with comprehensive edge cases
3. **test/test_*.c** (new) - new double-precision test files

I'll commit the test infrastructure changes together. Skipping `.claude/` and files outside the riscv directory.

input.command:
cd /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test: add double-precision test files and expand single-precision coverage

Add test/test_<func>.c for all double-precision functions (sin, cos, tan,
atan, asin, acos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.) to support opt vs ref comparison
via Makefile. Expand existing single-precision test_<func>f.c with structured
test sections (specials, tiny, denorms, ranges, edge cases).

Update Makefile to support double-precision assembly (s_*.s) opt vs ref builds,
split FUNCS_DOUBLE into FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT (with assembly) and FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT
(toolchain libm only), and extend size/clean targets to cover s_*.o objects.

Add plan.md and riscv32{,e}_opt.csv size tracking data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
